items.title: Border Force busts multi-million dollar illicit NT tobacco operation

children.content: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

gallery.altText: A box with black packages with four cigarette packets lined up on top with one pack with cigarettes poking out of one packet

thumbnail.altText: A box with black packages with four cigarette packets lined up on top with one pack with cigarettes poking out of one packet

items.title: Dieback disease killing mango trees is spreading in the NT

children.content: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

image.altText: Needles injected into mango trees

items.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed, report finds

children.content: Almost one-third of children in out-of-home care in the Northern Territory were allegedly harmed in the last financial year, according to a new report from the Office of the Children's Commissioner.

image.altText: Children's toys, shoes, coloring pens.

items.title: Teenage boy dies after stolen car rolls on major NT highway

children.content: A teenager has died after the stolen vehicle he was inside rolled on a major Northern Territory highway in the early hours of this morning.

gallery.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

thumbnail.altText: A vehicle parked across the highway, blocking traffic access, with a logo on the side that reads: ROAD OPERATIONS

items.title: Gas workers strike as negotiations on pay deal stall

children.content: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

image.altText: Evening / dusk shot of Darwin harbour, industrial site, with yellow lights glowing and cranes in view.

items.title: Residents still can't eat too many fish from river in PFAS-affected town

children.content: Katherine residents have welcomed a $2 billion lawsuit over PFAS contamination in the town, saying "it's about time we started holding large corporations to account".

image.altText: Anjali Palmer stands outside a medical clinic in Katherine.

items.title: These are the 'worst offenders' in Australian packaging

children.content: A trendy plastic-and-metal takeaway cup used by cafes for iced drinks has been named Australia's worst packaging in the first Unpackit Awards.

gallery.altText: Four dirty, empty can-shaped plastic drinking vessels with a metal pull tab top.

thumbnail.altText: Four dirty, empty can-shaped plastic drinking vessels with a metal pull tab top.

items.title: Snowy 2.0 blows out by more than $3m a day. Will it ever be worth it?

children.content: Australia's most ambitious renewable energy project is slowly nearing completion, but experts are divided on whether Snowy 2.0 will ever justify its snowballing price tag.

image.altText: A worker waters the ground in the distance while in a tunnel and a excavator works alongside them

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done

children.content: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

image.altText: Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll celebrates a goal with a shout in front of fans at Perth Stadium, in Fremantle's white jersey.

items.title: Former Canberra Liberals leader leaves party citing 'threat of physical assault'

children.content: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley says she has experienced "bullying, intimidation, lies and a threat of physical assault" in the past 18 months.

gallery.altText: A politician in a pink jacket stands in front of microphones.

thumbnail.altText: A politician in a pink jacket stands in front of microphones.

avatar.alt: Kate Midena, smiling at the camera.

items.title: Three used submarines a 'better arrangement' for Australia, minister says

children.content: The defence minister says the new AUKUS deal that will see Australia buy three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the US is what the government had wanted all along. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: The housing 'super cycle' may be ending, and that could be a good thing

children.content: Before the federal budget, the housing market was already starting to choke on its own excesses. Now some economists are tipping an extended period where home prices grow no faster than general inflation.

image.altText: A Queenslander house with an open home sign.

items.title: Kiwi greyhounds expected to be sent to Qld ahead of NZ racing ban

children.content: Australian officials say they will have no control over how the greyhounds are used once they arrive in Queensland.

gallery.altText: Three greyhounds that race on leads at a track in Victoria.

thumbnail.altText: Three greyhounds that race on leads at a track in Victoria.

items.title: Banned products in Australia sold on Temu, Amazon and AliExpresss

children.content: Products that may already be banned in Australia are being sold by retail giants including Amazon, Temu, AliExpress and eBay.

image.altText: A bunch of toys, including fake cigarettes displayed with a white background

items.title: Starmer condemns handcuffed teen's death as police response sparks protests

children.content: The British prime minister said he "felt sick" while watching video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

image.altText: The heads and hands of protesters, including one holding a white sign with a picture of a Caucasian teen.

items.title: Five ways to prevent mould in your home and the best ways to clean it

children.content: Winter is here and with it comes the increased risk of mould in homes, particularly in colder parts of the country. But there are simple ways to keep to it at bay.

gallery.altText: A gloved hand wipes a window sill with some mould in the cracks.

thumbnail.altText: A gloved hand wipes a window sill with some mould in the cracks.

items.title: This rapper was having panic attacks on stage, until he transitioned

children.content: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright and novelist Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

image.altText: Close-up of Kae Tempest looking down at sunset, with a city skyline in silhouette behind them.

items.title: Why Hugh Jackman's brother cried on his first day as a prison-school teacher

children.content: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

image.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.

items.title: 'Even sleep is expensive': The challenge for a people forced to live in ruins

children.content: It's been seven months since the ceasefire in Gaza, but reconstruction has barely begun, water remains contaminated and minimal aid is getting into the strip. This is what life is like for its residents.

gallery.altText: A man brushes another person's hair in a barber's chair.
Damaged buildings in the background and a large number of tents in the foreground in Gaza City.
Two women wearing hijabs crouching down on the ground and washing clothes in buckets.

thumbnail.altText: A man brushes another person's hair in a barber's chair.
Damaged buildings in the background and a large number of tents in the foreground in Gaza City.
Two women wearing hijabs crouching down on the ground and washing clothes in buckets.

items.title: Fertiliser imports hit 1 million tonne mark since Iran war began

children.content: Streamlined biosecurity checks of fertiliser shipments have fast-tracked imports of urea for Australia's winter crop.

image.altText: Container ships docked at Port Botany.

items.title: Australian men guilty after taking 'black flight' to flee country

children.content: Three Australian men have been found guilty by a District Court in Merauke after taking a "black flight" into Indonesia's South Papua province from Far North Queensland without visas.

image.altText: three men in white dress shirts and black pants sit in an Indonesian court

items.title: Trump names inexperienced ally as intelligence director

children.content: Bill Pulte, who is known for publicly attacking the US president's political enemies, would also retain his existing jobs overseeing federal housing and mortgage policies.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiles.

items.title: Heat-related deaths 'tip of the iceberg' in India as UN sends El Niño warning

children.content: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

image.altText: A man puts his head under a running outdoor tap.

items.title: Peabo Bryson, A Whole New World and Beauty and the Beast singer, dies

children.content: Peabo Bryson, known for his Grammy and Oscar-winning Disney collaborations, has died at 75.

gallery.altText: Peabo Bryson wearing a white suit and white framed reading glasses, holding a mic while surrounded by a crowd

thumbnail.altText: Peabo Bryson wearing a white suit and white framed reading glasses, holding a mic while surrounded by a crowd

items.title: 'Us duck hunters': Victorian watchdog accused of siding with shooters

children.content: The Game Management Authority is accused of of turning a blind eye to illegal behaviour by shooters while bullying wildlife rescuers and anti-hunting campaigners.

image.altText: A man with a shotgun over his shoulder carrying a gun in his other hand, up to his knees in water

items.title: 'Grossly negligent' UK Athletics fined for 'avoidable' death of Paralympian

children.content: Abdullah Hayayei, from the United Arab Emirates, was preparing for the 2017 World Paralympic Athletics Championships at the Newham Leisure Centre when a metal throwing cage fell on him.

image.altText: Abdullah Hayayei tosses a discus in the air

items.title: Renegades BBL team to be sold as new Victorian franchise established

children.content: The Melbourne Renegades BBL licence is set to be sold to a private investor, with Cricket Victoria to establish a new team, marking the end of the Stars as well.

gallery.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

thumbnail.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

items.title: Damning report questions what counts as a home in Tasmania's 10,000-home target

children.content: A financial review into Tasmania's soon-to-be-axed state housing authority says it relies too heavily on spreadsheets, and noted problems with transparency and effective decision-making.

image.altText: A shot of several half-built homes.

items.title: Driver found 'eating Maccas' in car that crashed into Sydney fountain

children.content: The CBD fountain sustained minimal damage after a French national allegedly drove into it early this morning.

image.altText: a car tilted into a fountain late at night

items.title: Australian-made rocket motor test fired for the first time

children.content: New vision has been released of the first test-firing, a step towards developing a greater Australian-made missile industry.

image.altText: A rocket booster igniting from an engine.

gallery.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

thumbnail.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

items.title: Coalition declares Labor's changes to Vic election laws 'unconstitutional'

children.content: Six months out from the state election, Victoria had no rules governing donations due to the High Court finding in April that the laws were unconstitutional.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan points while speaking in parliament.

items.title: Trump drops $2.5b 'weaponization' fund plans amid corruption allegations

children.content: The US president has abandoned plans for a $2.51 billion "anti-weaponization" fund for ‌victims of alleged government "weaponization", according to the acting attorney-general.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking during a cabinet meeting at the White House.

items.title: Enormous ship en route to pick up world's largest electric ferry

children.content: The 217-metre-long heavy-lift vessel Black Marlin will deliver a Tasmanian shipbuilder's new electric ferry to South America, after the previous transporter became caught up in the Strait of Hormuz closure.

gallery.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean

thumbnail.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean

items.title: Cancer patient's stem cell transplant struggle highlights need for more donors

children.content: Sarah Taylor is one of approximately 1,200 Australians urgently waiting for a life-saving stem cell transplant. Advocates say Australia's registry is "ridiculously small" compared to other nations, leading to potentially fatal consequences.

image.altText: A young woman cuddles a teddy bear as she sits in a hospital bed.

items.title: These companies have controversial pasts. Google AI didn't tell us

children.content: We searched Google for information on some of the world's biggest tobacco companies. The results raised questions about how much of the AI overview was coming from the brands themselves.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone where the user has googled tobacco company Philip Morris.
